interactables.name: Village Notice Board
interactables.text:
NOTICE: Crops failing across all fields. Lord Ashworth seeks able-bodied workers.
WARNING: Do not drink from the eastern well after dark.
MISSING: Three goats from the Miller farm. Last seen near Thornwood edge.
interactables.state_alt_text:
URGENT: Famine declared. All grain rationed by order of the Guard.
RIOTS: Market closed until further notice.
DEATH TOLL: Seven this week. May the Light preserve us.

interactables.name: Dead Songbirds
interactables.text: A cluster of dead songbirds lies near the well. Their beaks are stained black, as if they drank something foul. The water did this.

interactables.name: Village Well
interactables.text: The well water looks clear enough, but there's a faint chemical smell. Something isn't right.

state_conditions.alt_text: The well rope has been cut clean through — a deliberate act. Someone doesn't want water drawn from here anymore. The frayed end dangles uselessly into the dark.
You know now — nightcap root is poisoning the water supply. The source must be upstream, in Thornwood.

interactables.name: Herb Garden
interactables.text: Wild herbs grow in careful rows — someone tends this garden with love. You recognize feverfew and comfrey.
interactables.state_alt_text: Elara taught you which herbs to pick. You gather a handful of healing plants.

interactables.name: Chapel Door
interactables.text: The chapel door creaks open. Inside, rows of rough-hewn pews face a simple altar draped in gray cloth.

state_conditions.alt_text: Someone has scratched words into the chapel door in jagged letters: 'FALSE PROPHET.' The paint is fresh. Brother Maren has not cleaned it off. Perhaps he hasn't seen it. Perhaps he's leaving it as evidence.
The chapel is locked for the night. But you hear something from below — a low chanting...

interactables.name: Inn Sign
interactables.text:
THE ROTTING HARVEST
~ Established by Greta's grandmother ~
'We didn't choose the name. The name chose us.'

interactables.name: Weathered Graves
interactables.text: Ashworth family graves, dating back three centuries. Every thirty years, one grave is marked with a strange root-like symbol. The most recent is Lord Ashworth's father.

interactables.name: Grain Silo
interactables.text: Lord Ashworth's grain stores. The silo is heavily guarded — the last food reserve in Ashvale. A guard eyes you suspiciously.
interactables.state_alt_text: Charred ruins. The acrid smell of burnt grain hangs in the air. This was once Ashvale's last hope.

interactables.name: Old Barrel
interactables.text: An old barrel behind the inn. Inside you find some stale bread and a half-empty flask.

interactables.name: Abandoned Crate
interactables.text: A crate left behind when the market closed. Some rope and a rusty knife remain.

interactables.name: Dark Stain
interactables.text: Something dark stains the cobblestones near the manor road. It's been scrubbed, but not well enough. Someone was dragged from here.

interactables.name: Carved Tree
interactables.text: Deep carvings in the bark of an ancient oak. The symbols seem to writhe when you look at them directly. You can't read them.
interactables.state_alt_text: The talisman pulses warm against your chest. The carvings resolve into words: 'WHAT THE ROOTS TAKE, THE ROOTS KEEP. WHAT IS FREELY GIVEN, FREELY GROWS.' A covenant. A price.

interactables.name: Cold Campfire
interactables.text: The remains of a military campfire. Boot prints from at least twenty soldiers. Voss's company was here — professional, organized, and watching the village.

interactables.name: Sealed Decree
interactables.text:
A decree nailed to the manor gate in Lord Ashworth's shaking hand:
'By order of Lord Ashworth, no citizen shall enter the manor grounds until further notice. The lord is... indisposed. May the harvest endure.'
interactables.state_alt_text: The decree has been torn down. The manor gate stands open.

interactables.name: Well Bucket
interactables.text: You draw water from the well and examine it closely. It looks clear, but...
interactables.state_alt_text: You collect a water sample as Elara instructed. Under close inspection, you notice a faint purple tinge — nightcap root contamination.

interactables.name: Broken Chair
interactables.text: A sturdy wooden chair near the bar. Nothing remarkable.
interactables.state_alt_text: The chair is splintered, one leg snapped clean off. A dark stain on the floor nearby — blood or ale, hard to tell. Greta mentions a brawl broke out while you were away. 'Hungry men fight over nothing,' she says.

interactables.name: Garden Fence
interactables.text: A low wooden fence surrounds what was once a vegetable garden. The soil is dark and quiet.
interactables.state_alt_text: The fence is smashed flat. Hoof prints everywhere — deer, from the size of them. Whatever was growing here has been trampled into mud. The forest's animals are getting bolder, or more desperate.

interactables.name: Water Trough
interactables.text: A stone trough for livestock, filled with well water. A few chickens peck nearby.
interactables.state_alt_text: A dead cow lies on its side near the trough, bloated and stiff. Two more carcasses further on. The water in the trough has a faint purple sheen. The poison is killing more than crops now.

interactables.name: Loose Stone
interactables.text: A loose stone in the alley wall. Behind it, a small hollow — you find a pouch of lockpicks and a coded note.
interactables.state_alt_text: The hollow behind the stone is empty. Scratch marks suggest something was recently removed in a hurry. Fenrick has moved his stash. He's getting careful.

interactables.name: Old Maren's Bench
interactables.text: Old Maren's stone bench at the village edge. Herb bundles hang from the armrest, drying in the wind.
interactables.state_alt_text: The bench is empty. Scattered herbs lie on the ground as if dropped suddenly. A villager tells you Old Maren was found collapsed here this morning. She's been taken inside, but she's not well. Not well at all.

interactables.name: Crumpled Note
interactables.state_alt_text: A crumpled note on the inn floor in Elara's handwriting: 'You KNOW what's in the water, Greta. Stop pretending the inn is neutral ground.' Greta and Elara have had words. The alliance between healer and innkeeper is fraying.

interactables.name: Fresh Tracks
interactables.text: Muddy ground near the gate. The usual boot prints of guards and travelers.
interactables.state_alt_text: Unfamiliar boot prints in the mud — heavy, military-issue, entering the village. Harlan let someone in after dark. He won't meet your eyes when you ask about it.

interactables.name: Drawing in Dirt
interactables.text: Scuff marks in the dirt. Children play here during the day.
interactables.state_alt_text: A half-finished drawing in the dirt — root patterns, spiraling outward. A child's work, abandoned suddenly. You hear the relief in a mother's voice nearby: 'Don't you EVER wander off like that again!' The child was found at the forest edge, drawing.

interactables.name: Stable
interactables.text: A horse lies on its side in the stable, barely breathing. Its water trough is full — from the village well. The same poisoned water. Elara left herbs beside it, but there's only so much herb poultice can do against nightcap root.
interactables.state_alt_text: The horse is dead. Its eyes are still open, reflecting a sky it will never see again. Someone covered it with a blanket. Probably Elara.

interactables.name: Village Chickens
interactables.text: A handful of scraggly chickens peck at the cobblestones. They look as hungry as everyone else. There used to be two dozen. Now there are five.
interactables.state_alt_text: Three chickens left. Someone is eating them at night. Nobody admits it. Nobody blames them.

interactables.name: A Patient Dog
interactables.text: A brown mutt sits at the door of the cold forge, tail occasionally thumping the ground. Waiting. His name is Anchor — Harlan named him that. His real name is Bark. The blacksmith called him Bark.
interactables.state_alt_text: Anchor trots over and leans against your leg. He's chosen you now. The forge is cold. The blacksmith isn't coming back. But Anchor still checks the door every morning, just in case.
